# Figure description: 4_Карта_прогнозов_M-42-XIV.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-XIV/4_Карта_прогнозов_M-42-XIV.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geological forecast/predictive map** ("Карта прогнозов") with elements showing the spatial distribution patterns of mineral deposits. It is a thematic geological map compiled at **1:200,000 scale**, presented as a hand-drafted/printed document typical of Soviet-era geological survey output.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers sheet **М-42-XIV** of the standard Soviet topographic/geological grid system, located in northern Kazakhstan. It was compiled in **1984** by the Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association (Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение) under the Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR. The map is designated **Чертёж №35** (Drawing No. 35) and is registered in the Kazakh SSR Republican Geological Fund (**Инв. №** partially legible, approximately **26-222**). The compiler is listed as **Кириенко В.В.** (Kirienko V.V.), and the map was compiled at the Central Geological-Prospecting Expedition.

The subject area covers a geologically complex zone within the Central Kazakhstan fold belt, with Carboniferous, Devonian, Permian, and Cenozoic (KZ) rock units exposed or inferred across the sheet.

---

## Key Content

### Geological Units and Stratigraphy
The map displays a variety of geological formations differentiated by letter-number codes:

- **KZ** — Cenozoic (Quaternary/Neogene cover), shown as blank/white areas, widely distributed across the map, particularly in the western, central, and southern portions
- **C₁** — Lower Carboniferous rocks, distributed across the central and eastern portions
- **C₂** — Middle Carboniferous units, appearing in the eastern sector
- **C₃fr** — Upper Carboniferous (Frasne equivalent or local stratigraphic unit), visible in the eastern margin
- **D₃dr** / **D₃dv** — Upper Devonian units (likely Famennian or Frasnian), appearing in the central and southern parts
- **P₁kr** / **P₂kr** — Lower and Upper Permian units (possibly Kungurian), visible in the northern portion
- **R₁Ar** / **R₂Ar** — Riphean/Archean or Proterozoic units, appearing in the western-central area
- **C₁str**, **C₂bz**, **C₃ftr**, **D₃ba** — additional local stratigraphic subdivisions visible as labels on the map

### Structural Features
- Numerous **fault lines** are drawn across the map — both solid lines (established faults) and dashed lines (inferred faults), trending predominantly **NW–SE** and **NE–SW**, creating a conjugate fault pattern
- A prominent **diagonal structural zone** crosses the map from the northwest to the southeast, delineated by closely spaced red diagonal hatch lines — this appears to represent a **major tectonic boundary or predictive ore-bearing zone** (labeled **2Г** in two locations, upper-left and lower-central areas)
- The hatched zone labeled **2Г** likely designates a **prognostic resource category** (Category 2G = прогнозные ресурсы категории P₂ or similar Soviet classification), indicating an area of inferred mineral potential

### Mineralization and Ore Occurrences
- Several **filled circles (black dots)** mark known mineral occurrences or deposits — notably near the center-right of the map with associated numbers (e.g., **700**, **1000**, **1200**), likely representing **ore body numbers or resource tonnage estimates** in thousands of tonnes
- **Green elongated ellipses/lenses** scattered across the eastern portion of the map likely represent **known ore bodies or mineral showings** (possibly skarn-type or vein-type deposits)
- A **pinkish-red shaded area** near the center of the map (associated with label **1В** and annotations **Zn, Pb, Cu, Bi, O** — partially legible) represents a **known or inferred polymetallic ore zone** — this appears to be the primary deposit or occurrence being evaluated
- Near the central deposit zone, labels such as **Cu** (copper), **Pb** (lead), **Zn** (zinc) are visible, indicating **polymetallic mineralization** (Cu-Pb-Zn type)
- Contour lines with values **800**, **1000**, **1200** appear in the northern portion, likely representing **topographic contours** or **depth-to-basement isolines**

### Forecast Zones
- The bold diagonal hatched band (red diagonal lines, labeled **2Г**) cuts diagonally across the entire map from NW to SE — this is the central predictive feature, delineating a **prospective belt** for mineral exploration
- A smaller secondary hatched zone is present in the **lower-center and bottom** of the map, also labeled **2Г**, suggesting continuation of the same prospective belt southward
